title: The Zero Hour at Cantigny
description: May 28, 1918. The American 1st Division prepares to seize the German-held village of Cantigny in the AEF's first major offensive. But as the creeping barrage begins, evidence emerges that someone has sabotaged the forward wire network and falsified the artillery timing tables — and if the culprit is not exposed before the German counter-barrage falls, the men holding the heights will be slaughtered.

title: Titanic: The Final Hours
description: It is 11:40 PM on April 14, 1912, and the RMS Titanic has struck an iceberg at 41°43'N 49°56'W. Three crew members — a wireless operator, a second engineer, and a first class steward — must coordinate across the sinking ship to keep the lights burning, the signals transmitting, and the lifeboats filled before the Atlantic claims her. Every decision has a price.

title: Sargasso Deep: The Three Keys
description: October 27, 1962. The Soviet submarine B-59 lies crippled and suffocating beneath the Sargasso Sea, hunted by an American destroyer task force. Three officers — a flotilla chief who must hold back his key, a sonar analyst who must read the difference between signals and death, and an American captain who must compel surrender without triggering catastrophe — have ninety minutes to prevent a nuclear launch that would end the world.

title: Singing Wires
description: In the autumn of 1853, a red fire message races across Ezra Cornell's telegraph lines naming a veteran Underground Railroad conductor and his current charges. His son, a lightning flinger at the Sandusky junction, must use the enemy's own brass to misdirect the hunt while his father decides whether to trust the technology of slaveholders to save the lives in his care.

title: Midnight Errand: Boston, 1775
description: On the eve of the Regulars' march, a printer's apprentice must decide whether to carry a secret dispatch through a city on the edge of war. Every choice tonight will echo for years — but the clock strikes ten in less than an hour.

title: Lightning and the Midnight Coach
description: In the autumn of 1856, a red fire message crackles across Ezra Cornell's expanding telegraph lines, carrying a bounty description of freedom-seekers moving through the Ohio corridor. A veteran Underground Railroad conductor and his son — a telegraph operator at the Sandusky junction — must outmaneuver the brass and the law before the iron horse delivers its human cargo into the hands of slave catchers.

title: Greensboro Four: The Color Line
description: February 1, 1960, 4:30 PM. Four Black college freshmen occupy the segregated lunch counter at F.W. Woolworth's in Greensboro, North Carolina, and ask to be served. The store will not serve them. They will not leave. The next ninety minutes will determine whether the sit-in holds its moral integrity — and whether a single act of disciplined stillness can survive the gathering pressure of a hostile crowd, a calculating store management, and the weight of history.
roles: The Point Man — Ezell Blair Jr.

title: Dog Green: The Longest Morning
description: June 6, 1944, 0630 hours. The ramps of the landing craft are dropping on the deadliest stretch of Omaha Beach. Three men — a coxswain, a Ranger sergeant, and a battalion medic — must navigate chaos, impossible choices, and the rising tide to break through the Atlantic Wall at the Vierville Draw before the second wave drowns in the same blood as the first.

title: Bornholmer Straße: The First Breach
description: November 9, 1989. A fumbled press conference has ignited East Berlin. Three people — a border commander, a student protester, and a Western journalist — must navigate the next ninety minutes as twenty thousand citizens press against a gate that history will open, but that could just as easily become a massacre. The outcome is not yet written.

title: The Artesian Height
description: Western Springs, Illinois, summer 1892. The village's landmark stone water tower is days from completion when a bound drill string, a failed pressure test, and whispers of bond fraud threaten to bury the entire project — and one engineer's reputation — two thousand feet underground. Three perspectives. One crisis. Thirty minutes to hold the line.

title: Apollo 13: Lifeboat
description: April 13, 1970: an oxygen tank has exploded 200,000 miles from Earth, and three men must survive four days in a spacecraft never designed for survival. Three perspectives — commander, flight director, command module pilot — must coordinate across silence, cold, and failing systems to bring the crew home alive.
